rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes for port-level contracts, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use visible feature names and legal identifiers when defining assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: When defining bounds or ranges in AGREE, use named constants or visible relations for clarity and maintainability.
Ensure that all referenced identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible within the scope of the annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use boolean expressions to define range conditions, e.g., eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;
Structure output envelope guarantees to include clamping behavior and limit flags for clarity.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts; use valid variables or outputs instead.
Do not place implementation-specific assumptions or guarantees in component type scopes.
